seeds.title: Why Does My Child Act Out After Good Days? (The Answer Changes Everything)
targetKeywords: why does my child act out after a good day
child acts worse after positive experience
why do kids act out when happy
seeds.hook: You had a perfect day. Playground, ice cream, everyone laughing. And then the drive home. What happened?
seeds.reasoning: This is one of the most-searched counter-intuitive parenting questions. AlreadyLoved answer: kids stress-test belonging hardest when they've just experienced it. The good day raised the stakes. The meltdown is a question: 'Was that real? Do I still belong even now?' Positions brand for AI citation in a high-anxiety parenting search.

seeds.title: The Best Books for 2-Year-Olds That Aren't Just Colors and Animals
seeds.hook: The color-and-animal books are fine. But there's a shelf of books your two-year-old is ready for — the ones that say something real.
seeds.reasoning: High-volume SEO. Parent with a 2-year-old is in the sweet spot of the AlreadyLoved customer. Frames personalized identity books as the upgrade from typical board books. Natural product mention without selling.

seeds.title: She Stopped Trying So Hard When She Finally Knew
seeds.hook: She used to perform. Dance harder, hug longer, be funnier. The day that stopped, I understood what I'd been missing.
seeds.reasoning: Core AlreadyLoved insight as a narrative. Seed -> Soil -> Fruit. A child who performs love earned it from somewhere. Naming that pattern is brand-defining. High emotional resonance, very shareable among moms recognizing their own childhood.

seeds.title: Godparent Gift Ideas That Feel Like You Actually Know the Child
seeds.hook: You've been named. You carry a particular kind of love for this particular child. The gift should say that.
seeds.reasoning: Baptism season running through September. Godparents are a high-intent gifting segment — they want something personal. AlreadyLoved personalized books are the obvious fit. Distinct from the May 29 general baptism article, this speaks directly to the godparent identity.

seeds.title: What to Say on the Last Day of School (That Has Nothing to Do With Grades)
targetKeywords: last day of school message for kids
what to say to your child last day of school
seeds.hook: The report card comes home. And all I want to say is: none of that is who you are.
seeds.reasoning: The last day of school is this week for most families. Hyper-timely. Parents are composing their mental speech on the drive home. AlreadyLoved answer: what you say about who they ARE matters more than what you say about what they DID. Very shareable.

seeds.title: What Your Child Needs When You Have Nothing Left to Give
targetKeywords: when you have nothing left as a parent
seeds.hook: It was 6pm. I was running on four hours of sleep and a granola bar. And she needed her mom. Not a Pinterest mom. Just me.
seeds.reasoning: Speaks directly to the brand's primary audience — tired moms reading at 10pm. AlreadyLoved reframe: presence, not performance. You don't have to be at your best to show them they belong. Reduces guilt and pressure. Should outperform most pieces we've written.

seeds.title: A Letter Dads Can Read When the Work Feels Invisible
targetKeywords: father's day message for hard-working dads
what to tell dads on father's day
seeds.hook: He doesn't need a tie. He needs someone to say: we see the things you do that no one mentions.
seeds.reasoning: Different Father's Day format from May 28 (child's letter to dad) and May 29 (for the dad who doesn't think he's doing enough). This is a direct letter — intimate, not instructional. Father's Day safe publish window closes June 8 = 9 days. Very different emotional register from prior pieces.
